com.mentor.chs.plugin

Interface IXTabPanel

  • All Known Subinterfaces:
    IXDesignTabPanel, IXFunctionDesignInspectionPanel, IXFunctionDesignTabPanel, IXHarnessDesignTabPanel, IXHarnessInspectionPanel, IXInspectionPanel, IXIntegratorDesignTabPanel, IXIntegratorInspectionPanel, IXLogicDesignTabPanel, IXLogicInspectionPanel, IXOutputTabPanel, IXTopologyDesignTabPanel, IXTopologyInspectionPanel

    @NotToBeImplementedDirectly
public interface IXTabPanel
    The base panel provider inteface to be used for Output window, inspection panal or design tab capabilities.

    This interface represents a panel that a developer may implement to represent a particular view of data that they require.

    See Also:
    IXOutputTabPanel, IXInspectionPanel, IXDesignTabPanel

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      void destroy()
      This method asks the panel to free any resources it might hold and prepare itself for disposal.
      javax.swing.JPanel initialize(IXApplicationContext context)
      This method asks the panel to initialize itself using the given context and return a JPanel that is to be added to the Tabbed pane of the client and shown to the user.

    • Method Detail

      • initialize

        javax.swing.JPanel initialize(IXApplicationContext context)
        This method asks the panel to initialize itself using the given context and return a JPanel that is to be added to the Tabbed pane of the client and shown to the user.

        The context may be queried for any details of current project data or selection data as required.

        This call will be made prior to the panel being made visible, it is permissible for this operation to take some time but the JPanel should not be returned until all initialzation is complete.

        Immediately after an initialize( context ) call a panel may expect to have its content updated. The panel which implements different change listeners is expected to update the panel accordingly when it recieves the change events.

        Parameters:
        context - , the IXApplicationContext representing the current state of the application
        Returns:
        JPanel, the JPanel to be displayed to the user initialized with any data required.

      • destroy

        void destroy()
        This method asks the panel to free any resources it might hold and prepare itself for disposal.

        This is called when the panel is being closed so the panel will no longer be visible.
